title: Education Workshop System
description: Structured, editorial design system for courses, agentic AI workshops, tutorials, and lesson materials.

typography_tokens.heading: Source Sans 3, Noto Sans KR, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.body: Source Sans 3, Noto Sans KR, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.label: Source Sans 3, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.caption: Source Sans 3, system-ui, sans-serif

PrimaryButton.description: Primary action for lesson start, registration, and submission moments.
PrimaryButton.usage: Use once per decision area; keep secondary actions neutral.

LessonCard.description: Course module card with visible hierarchy and practical outcome.

preview.html:
Module 01

Agent Workflow Design

Map steps, gates, and expected outputs.
preview.css: .lesson-card{border-left:4px solid #A0522D;border-radius:8px;border:1px solid #D8CCB8;padding:18px;background:#fff}.lesson-card span{font:700 12px IBM Plex Mono;color:#6B665E}.lesson-card h3{margin:8px 0 4px;font-size:20px}.lesson-card p{margin:0;color:#6B665E}

LessonCard.usage: Use for workshop modules, practice blocks, and resource cards.

TextField.description: Readable labeled input for quiz, registration, and feedback forms.
TextField.usage: Always pair with visible labels and helper or validation text.

guidelines_markdown:
## Principles

- Make learning hierarchy visible: module, objective, task, output.
- Use the rust accent sparingly for primary actions and section markers.
- Keep body copy practical and outcome-oriented.
- Use code and rubrics as first-class content, not decorative blocks.
skill_markdown: Use this design system for educational websites, AI workshop kits, course materials, slide support pages, tutorials, and lesson dashboards.

title: Developer Workbench System
description: Dense, high-signal system for CLI tools, MCP products, API docs, SDK dashboards, and automation builders.

typography_tokens.heading: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.label: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.caption: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.mono: JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, ui-monospace, monospace

CommandButton.description: Compact command-oriented action button for developer workflows.
CommandButton.usage: Use for concrete commands. Avoid vague CTA copy.

CodeBlock.description: Copyable code or terminal surface with strong contrast.
CodeBlock.usage: Always include copy affordance in production UI.

ConfigField.description: Labeled configuration input for keys, URLs, and command parameters.
ConfigField.usage: Use mono font for machine values and visible validation for invalid endpoints.

guidelines_markdown:
## Principles

- Prioritize command clarity, logs, copyable code, and status visibility.
- Keep motion minimal and state transitions explicit.
- Use the green accent for successful execution and primary command moments.
- Do not hide important configuration inside decorative cards.
skill_markdown: Use this design system for developer tooling, API products, MCP dashboards, CLI control panels, SDK documentation, and automation workbenches.

title: SaaS Operator Console
description: Quiet, durable interface system for SaaS admin panels, workflow registries, CRM-style tools, and repeated operations.

ActionButton.usage: Use short verb labels. Reserve danger variant for destructive actions.

DataTable.description: Dense table for repeated scanning, comparison, and batch actions.

preview.css: .ops-table{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font:13px Source Sans 3}.ops-table th,.ops-table td{border-bottom:1px solid #E4E4E7;padding:10px;text-align:left}.ops-table th{color:#71717A}

DataTable.usage: Always provide loading, empty, error, populated, and edge states.

SearchField.description: Filtering input for list and table surfaces.
SearchField.usage: Use on every long registry or table view.

guidelines_markdown:
## Principles

- Favor dense, predictable layouts over decorative marketing composition.
- Use accent color for action, focus, and active navigation only.
- Every table or list surface needs loading, empty, error, populated, and edge states.
- Keep card radius at 8px or less.
skill_markdown: Use this design system for SaaS dashboards, admin consoles, CRM tools, workflow registries, settings pages, and operational products.

title: Data Cockpit Dark
description: Dark, instrumented system for analytics, monitoring, finance, security, and live operational dashboards.

typography_tokens.display: IBM Plex Sans, Geist, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.heading: IBM Plex Sans, Geist, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.body: IBM Plex Sans, Geist, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.label: IBM Plex Sans, Geist, system-ui, sans-serif
typography_tokens.caption: IBM Plex Sans, Geist, system-ui, sans-serif

MetricTile.description: Compact KPI tile with value, trend, and status cue.
MetricTile.usage: Use for one metric per tile; avoid mixing unrelated units.

FilterSelect.description: Compact select control for dashboard filtering.
FilterSelect.usage: Keep filters near charts and preserve selected state visibility.

AlertPanel.description: High-contrast alert panel for monitoring incidents.
AlertPanel.usage: Use sparingly for actionable incident states.

guidelines_markdown:
## Principles

- Preserve contrast and chart readability before adding decoration.
- Use amber or cyan for scanning signals; do not flood the viewport with accent.
- Every data panel must document loading, empty, error, populated, and edge states.
- Keep labels literal and figures in mono where precision matters.
skill_markdown: Use this design system for analytics, observability, security monitoring, finance dashboards, and live operations views.
